news_app.admin module¶

class news_app.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

list_display = ('title', 'publisher', 'journalist', 'is_approved', 'created_at')¶
list_filter = ('is_approved', 'publisher', 'created_at')¶
property media¶
search_fields = ('title', 'summary', 'body', 'journalist__username')¶
class news_app.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: UserAdmin

fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Role', {'fields': ('role',)}), ('Reader subscriptions', {'fields': ('subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))¶
filter_horizontal = ('subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')¶
list_display = ('username', 'email', 'role', 'is_staff', 'is_active')¶
property media¶
class news_app.admin.PublisherAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

filter_horizontal = ('editors', 'journalists')¶
list_display = ('name', 'website', 'created_at')¶
property media¶
search_fields = ('name',)¶

news_app.api_urls module¶

news_app.api_views module¶

news_app.api_views.article_detail_api(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

GET /api/articles/<id>/

Reader only endpoint. Returns a single approved article, only if it matches the authenticated Reader’s subscriptions (publisher OR journalist).

If not allowed, return 404.

news_app.api_views.my_feed(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Reader only endpoint. Returns approved articles, filtered by the authenticated Reader’s subscriptions: - subscribed publishers - subscribed journalists.

news_app.apps module¶

class news_app.apps.NewsAppConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]¶

Bases: AppConfig

name = 'news_app'¶

news_app.forms module¶

class news_app.forms.ArticleForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelForm

Form for journalists to create/edit an article.

  • only expose fields the journalist should type.

  • journalist and is_approved are controlled in the view.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['publisher', 'title', 'summary', 'body']¶
model¶

alias of Article

widgets = {'body': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>, 'summary': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>}¶
base_fields = {'body': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.models.ModelChoiceField object>, 'summary': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
clean_body()[source]¶
clean_title()[source]¶
declared_fields = {}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

Publisher create form (Editor only view will use this). - name required - website optional

news_app.models module¶

class news_app.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

An article written by a journalist. - Must indicate whether the article has been approved by an editor or not.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

updated_at¶

class news_app.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model with role field.

  • Role selects which Group the user is placed into: Readers / Editors / Journalists.

  • Subscriptions only for Readers. Django ManyToMany fields cannot be None, so we enforce “empty” vs “used”.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('editor', 'Editor'), ('journalist', 'Journalist')]¶
ROLE_EDITOR = 'editor'¶
ROLE_JOURNALIST = 'journalist'¶
ROLE_READER = 'reader'¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

email¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

first_name¶

get_next_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
get_role_display(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.CharField: role>)¶
groups¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

independent_articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

A news publisher. - A publisher can have multiple editors and journalists.

news_app.tests module¶

class news_app.tests.ApiTests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

API tests for: - GET /api/me/feed/ - GET /api/articles/<id>/

Auth method: BasicAuth.

auth_as(username: str, password: str)[source]¶

Sets the BasicAuth header for the API client.

clear_auth()[source]¶

Clears auth for testing 401 responses.

setUp()[source]¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_detail_non_reader_forbidden()[source]¶
test_detail_reader_allowed_returns_200()[source]¶
test_detail_reader_not_subscribed_returns_404()[source]¶
test_detail_reader_subscribed_but_unapproved_returns_404()[source]¶
test_detail_requires_auth()[source]¶
test_feed_non_reader_forbidden()[source]¶
test_feed_reader_journalist_subscription_returns_matching_approved()[source]¶
test_feed_reader_no_subscriptions_empty()[source]¶
test_feed_reader_publisher_subscription_returns_match_approved_only()[source]¶
test_feed_reader_union_of_subscriptions()[source]¶
test_feed_requires_auth()[source]¶

news_app.urls module¶

news_app.views module¶

news_app.views.article_create(request)[source]¶

Journalist only. Articles created here are always drafts (is_approved=False).

news_app.views.article_delete(request, article_id)[source]¶

Journalist only. Only allow deleting own articles. If approved, deleting is blocked.

news_app.views.article_detail(request, article_id)[source]¶

Reader detail page. Only allow access to approved articles (if not approved, 404).

news_app.views.article_edit(request, article_id)[source]¶

Journalist only. Only allow editing on your own articles. If approved, editing is blocked.

news_app.views.article_list(request)[source]¶

Reader list page. Only show approved articles.

news_app.views.dashboard(request)[source]¶
news_app.views.editor_approve_article(request, article_id)[source]¶

Editor only. Approves an article (is_approved=True) then: - emails subscribers (publisher + journalist subscriptions) - posts to X No signals.

Even if email/X fails, the approval still succeeds.

news_app.views.editor_approve_newsletter(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶

Editor only. Approves a newsletter (is_approved=True) then: - emails subscribers (publisher + journalist subscriptions) - posts to X, best effort. No signals.

Even if email/X fails, approval still succeeds.

news_app.views.editor_article_delete(request, article_id)[source]¶

Editor only. Can delete allowed articles.

news_app.views.editor_article_edit(request, article_id)[source]¶

Editor only. Can edit allowed articles. Approved stays approved.

news_app.views.editor_articles(request)[source]¶

Editor only. List of articles editor is allowed to manage.

news_app.views.editor_can_manage_publisher_item(editor_user, publisher)[source]¶

Returns True if editor_user is allowed to manage content for this publisher. - If publisher is independent: any editor allowed - If publisher exists: editor must be assigned to it

news_app.views.editor_newsletter_delete(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶

Editor only. Can delete allowed newsletters.

news_app.views.editor_newsletter_edit(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶

Editor only. Can edit allowed newsletters. Approved stays approved.

news_app.views.editor_newsletters(request)[source]¶

Editor only. List of newsletters editor is allowed to manage.

news_app.views.editor_pending_articles(request)[source]¶

Editor only. Shows all articles that are not approved yet.

news_app.views.editor_pending_newsletters(request)[source]¶

Editor only. Shows all newsletters that are not approved yet.

news_app.views.home(request)[source]¶
news_app.views.journalist_list(request)[source]¶

Reader list of all journalist users. Filter CustomUser by role to only show journalists.

news_app.views.journalist_subscribe(request, journalist_id)[source]¶
news_app.views.journalist_unsubscribe(request, journalist_id)[source]¶
news_app.views.login_view(request)[source]¶
news_app.views.logout_view(request)[source]¶
news_app.views.my_articles(request)[source]¶

Journalist only. Shows the logged-in journalist’s own articles, approved or not.

news_app.views.my_newsletters(request)[source]¶

Journalist only. Shows the logged-in journalist’s own newsletters, approved or not.

news_app.views.newsletter_create(request)[source]¶

Journalist only. Newsletters created here are always drafts (is_approved=False).

news_app.views.newsletter_delete(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶

Journalist only. Only allow deleting own newsletters. If approved, deleting is blocked.

news_app.views.newsletter_detail(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶
news_app.views.newsletter_edit(request, newsletter_id)[source]¶

Journalist only. Only allow editing on own newsletters. If approved, editing is blocked.

news_app.views.newsletter_list(request)[source]¶
news_app.views.publisher_create(request)[source]¶

Editor only. Allows editors to add a new Publisher from the normal UI (not admin).

news_app.views.publisher_list(request)[source]¶

Reader list of all publishers.

news_app.views.publisher_subscribe(request, publisher_id)[source]¶
news_app.views.publisher_unsubscribe(request, publisher_id)[source]¶
news_app.views.register_view(request)[source]¶
news_app.views.user_is_reader(user)[source]¶

Role check.

Module contents¶

News App Consolidation
